description: Outside, a brick apartment building stands at a street corner. The setting changes to an apartment kitchen. Ross, Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe are seated around a table, while Rachel stands pouring juice. Chandler holds a brown leather wallet.
Phoebe looks at the wallet and asks, "New?"
Chandler opens the wallet.

Chandler gestures with one hand while holding the wallet in the other. "Oh, yeah, it was time. The old condom ring in the leather just doesn't say cool anymore, you know?" he says. Audience laughter is audible. Rachel turns toward the refrigerator and places a carton inside.
Rachel closes the refrigerator door. Monica stands up from the table.

Monica points toward the refrigerator and says, "Rachel, you just put an empty carton back in the fridge." Rachel turns around and replies, "Oh, yeah, I know, but the garbage was full." Audience laughter is heard.
Monica opens the refrigerator and takes out the carton.
Holding the carton, Monica looks at Rachel and asks, "Have you ever taken out the trash?" Rachel replies, "Yeah,"
Monica crushes the carton in her hands and walks toward the trash can.
Monica pulls a white trash bag from a bin and says, "since you've lived here." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel looks at the others at the table. Monica hands Rachel the trash bag and a flat cardboard box.
Rachel accepts the items and says, "Well, I thought you liked doing it," with her pitch rising at the end. Audience laughter is audible. She turns and walks toward the apartment door.
Rachel stops at the door, holding the trash bag and the cardboard box. She turns back toward Monica.
Monica gestures toward the door and says, "Third door on the left."
Rachel nods, says, "Right," and opens the door.
The setting changes to a hallway. Mr. Treeger stands in front of an open trash chute, repeatedly pushing a broom handle inside. A sign on the wall reads "NO DISPOSAL OF HAZARDOUS MATERIALS!"
Mr. Treeger continues jabbing the broom handle into the chute, creating a loud metallic clanging sound. Rachel walks into the hallway, holding the trash bag and a pizza box.
Rachel stops and says, "Oh, hey, Mr."
Rachel finishes her greeting, saying, "Tree."
Mr. Treeger turns toward Rachel, leaning on the broom handle, and exhales audibly.
Mr. Treeger says, "It" while looking at Rachel.

Rachel steps forward and lifts the white trash bag, attempting to push it into the partially blocked chute. The bag makes a crinkling sound. Mr. Treeger stands beside her, resting his hands on the broom handle.
The trash bag slides into the chute. Rachel then turns the flat pizza box horizontally and tries to push it into the small opening.
Rachel uses both hands to push the pizza box further into the chute, but it does not fit. She lets go, and the box remains wedged in the opening. She steps back and brushes her hands together.
Rachel turns her head toward Mr. Treeger.
Mr. Treeger looks at Rachel and asks, "what are you doing?"
Rachel raises her hands slightly and stutters, her mouth opening and closing without speaking.
Rachel says, "Oh," and turns back to the chute.
Rachel reaches out and pulls the pizza box out of the chute.
Rachel holds the pizza box, opens the lid slightly, and says, "sorry. A little old, but"
Audience laughter is heard. Rachel looks inside the pizza box. Mr. Treeger points a finger at the box.
Mr. Treeger's volume rises as he says, "you're clogging up the chute. I just spent a half hour unclogging."
Rachel's shoulders rise as she says, "I'm sorry."
Rachel continues looking at Mr. Treeger, holding the box.
Rachel says, "I don't come in here a lot." Mr. Treeger replies without pausing, "Yeah, of course you don't, because you're a little princess."
Rachel stares at Mr. Treeger. Audience laughter is audible.
Pitching his voice higher, Mr. Treeger says, "Daddy, buy me a pizza. Daddy, buy me a candy factory." Audience laughter is audible.
Continuing in the higher pitch, Mr. Treeger says, "Daddy, make the cast of Cats sing Happy Birthday to me." Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel's jaw drops slightly as she listens.
Returning to his normal pitch and pointing at his chest, Mr. Treeger says, "What, you think you can just come in here, make a mess, a big man in the coveralls, or come and clean it up?"
Mr. Treeger leans forward slightly and says, "Well, why don't you think of someone else for a change?"
Rachel lowers her gaze, her lips pressed tightly together. Mr. Treeger stands still, watching her.
Rachel turns toward the hallway door, her voice trembling slightly as she says, "Okay, I'm sorry."
Rachel walks quickly out of the hallway, carrying the pizza box.
The setting changes back to the apartment. Joey sits on a sofa reading a newspaper while Ross sits beside him. Rachel walks through the door, still holding the pizza box.
Rachel places the pizza box on a small table. Monica walks toward her and says, "My god, you're going to cry about it?"
Rachel covers her mouth with one hand, breathing heavily as tears become visible on her face. An audience reaction is audible.
The screen turns completely black. No sound is audible.
A city skyline with tall buildings and an archway under a blue sky is visible. Instrumental transition music plays.
The setting changes back to the apartment kitchen. Joey stands near Rachel, who is seated at the table holding a yellow cloth to her face. Chandler, Monica, Phoebe, and Ross are also present.
Joey leans toward Rachel and asks, "Whoa, whoa. Treeger made you cry?" Rachel, wiping her nose with the cloth, replies, "Yes."
Her voice strained, Rachel says, "He said really mean things that were only partly true." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey stands up straight.

Joey points toward the door and says, "I'm gonna go down there and teach that guy a lesson." Monica raises her hand and says, "Joey, please don't do that. I think it's best if we just forget about it. That's-"
Rachel lowers the cloth from her face and says, "easy for you to say. You weren't almost just killed." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey nods and turns toward the apartment door.
Joey points a finger toward the door and says, "All right, that's it. School is in session."
Joey opens the door and exits the apartment. Chandler is seen looking toward the door.
In the apartment, Monica stands while Chandler sits at the table. Rachel, Ross, and Phoebe are seated nearby.
Monica looks at a small card in her hand and asks, "Is this a gym card?" Chandler takes the card from her and says, "Oh, yeah, gym member."
Chandler holds the card and continues, "I try to go four times a week, but I've missed the last"
Chandler adds, "1200 times." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica smiles and holds her hand out, taking the card back from Chandler.
Monica asks, "So why don't you quit?"
Chandler looks at Monica.
Chandler gestures with his right hand and says, "You don't think I've tried?" Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler pushes his chair back and stands up.
Chandler gestures with both hands as he speaks, "You think I like having $50 taken out of my bank account every month?" Ross sits on the couch armrest.
Chandler steps slightly forward.
Chandler gestures downwards, "No, they make you go all the way down there."
Chandler leans forward, moving his hands, "Then they use all these phrases and peppiness to try to confuse you."
Chandler pauses, keeping his hands raised.
Chandler points downward with one finger, "And then they bring out Maria."
Ross leans forward slightly.
Ross asks, "Who is Maria?" Chandler looks away and says, "Oh, Maria."
Chandler looks forward again, his gaze steady.
Chandler says, "You can't say no to her. She's like this lycra, spandex covered, gym treat." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross turns his head to look at Chandler.
Ross asks, "Do you need me to go down there with you and hold your hand?"
Chandler replies, "No." Ross says, "All right, so you're strong enough to face her alone." Chandler looks at Ross, "Oh, no. You'll have to come." Audience laughter is audible.
The scene changes to a hallway. Joey approaches a door labeled "MAINTENANCE SUPERVISOR" and knocks on it.
The door opens, revealing Mr. Treeger inside. Joey places his hands on his hips.
Mr. Treeger says, "Treeger. Hold on. I'll get the plunger," and turns away from the door.
Joey points his finger towards Mr. Treeger.
Joey says, "Hey, you hold on, pal. Now, you made my friend Rachel cry."
Joey points behind himself with his thumb, "So now you're going to go up there and you're going to"
Joey continues, "apologize to her. Unless you want me to call the landlord." Mr. Treeger steps forward, "And tell him what?"
Joey gestures with his hands, "Maybe you haven't heard of a little something called not making girls cry." Mr. Treeger puts his hands on his hips, "Yeah, or maybe you haven't heard a"
Mr. Treeger says, "little something called the Rent Stabilization Act of 1968."
Joey crosses his arms and says, "I have actually not heard of that." Audience laughter is audible.
Mr. Treeger keeps his hands on his hips, looking at Joey.
Mr. Treeger says, "Yeah, well, your friends are in violation of it."
Mr. Treeger continues, "I've been a nice guy up until now, but I don't need this grief. I'm going to"
Mr. Treeger points upward, "call the landlord and tell him that Monica is illegally subletting her grandmother's apartment."
Mr. Treeger stares at Joey.
Mr. Treeger leans slightly forward and says, "Your friends are out of here, pal," then steps back into the room.
Joey turns away, throwing his hands down.
Joey points back over his shoulder and says, "Don't you tell me something I don't know." He walks away. Audience laughter is audible.
The door closes. The scene changes back to the apartment. Phoebe sits at the table, looking at the food. Ross walks past in the background.
Phoebe rubs her forehead and says, "Oh, please, somebody tell me. I don't have to go to work today." Monica, seated next to her, asks, "What's the matter?"
Phoebe gestures with her hands, "My first massage is this incredibly gorgeous guy,"
Phoebe continues, "and every time I see him, I just want to do things to him that I'm not allowed"
Phoebe finishes, "to charge for." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica turns her head to look at Phoebe.
Monica says, "Do them for free."
Phoebe stands up, picking up a green jacket from the couch.
Phoebe starts putting the jacket on and says, "Oh, no, it is."
Phoebe continues sliding her arm into the sleeve of the green jacket.
Phoebe says, "No, no. Mrs."
Phoebe adjusts the jacket on her shoulders, "Potter fires people for messing around with clients, and it's against my oath as a masseuse."
Ross lowers his briefcase slightly.
Ross steps forward, holding a briefcase, and asks, "They make you take an oath?"
Phoebe presses both hands to her chest, "No, I made myself take one."
Phoebe lowers her hands, "Yeah, no fooling around with clients and always be prepared."
Phoebe smiles slightly.
Phoebe gestures with one hand, "Yeah, that one's actually from the Boy Scouts, but it just makes good sense." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross and Chandler watch Phoebe.
Ross asks, "Why don't you just give them to somebody else?" Phoebe turns and walks towards the door, "No, I can handle it. No, I'm a professional."
Phoebe walks past the coffee table. Monica looks down toward Phoebe's feet.
Monica points down and asks, "Oh, Phoebe, is that a new ankle bracelet?"
Phoebe stops walking.
Monica continues, "Well and you got a pedicure. Your feet are all dressed up"
Phoebe looks down at her own feet.
Chandler points at Phoebe and says, "because that's the only part of you he can see when he's on the table."
Phoebe looks up with a slight smile.
Monica points at Phoebe, "You're going to do some feet flirting?" Phoebe shakes her head, "I don't know what you're talking about."
Phoebe turns to walk toward the door again.
Ross asks, "Then how do you explain the toe ring?"
Phoebe stops near the door and turns back.
Phoebe says, "Because it's Arabian Princess Day at work. Okay. Leave me alone," and exits through the apartment door.
Ross and Chandler look toward the closed door. The scene shifts to an exterior shot of the apartment building.
The scene returns to the apartment. The door opens and Joey enters. Rachel stands up from the couch.
Rachel walks toward Joey and asks, "My hero. What happened?"
Joey gestures with his hands, "Well, I went down there and I told him no one treats my friends like that, and he better come up here and apologize."
Joey lowers his hands.
Joey turns to walk away, saying, "I'll see you later." Rachel reaches out, "Wait a minute."
Joey stops and turns back toward Rachel.
In the apartment living room, Monica stands facing Joey near the open door, while Rachel stands beside her. "What'd he say?" Monica asks.
Joey raises his hands slightly.
"He said that he wasn't going to apologize because you guys are living here illegally. So instead, what he's going to" Joey speaks, gesturing with his left hand.
"do is have you evicted. I'll see you later." Joey drops his hand and turns toward the hallway.
Monica steps forward, her mouth open.
"You got him evicted? I told you not to go down there." Monica speaks loudly, raising her hands.
"He made Rachel cry." Joey points his thumb at Rachel.
"Rachel always cries." Monica gestures with both hands.
Rachel brings both hands over her mouth and nose.
"That's not true." Rachel speaks through her hands, her voice trembling slightly.
Monica points her finger at Joey, stepping closer to him.
"You go down there and you suck up to him. I mean, you suck like you've never sucked before." Monica speaks, pointing her finger at Joey's chest.
Joey looks at Monica, opening his mouth slightly.
"All right, I'll try. But if I can't, you stay with Chandler and I till you get settled." Joey speaks, gesturing with both hands.
"Go." Monica interrupts, pointing toward the door.
"All right." Joey turns and steps out the door.
Joey stops, holding the edge of the door.
"I mean, I'll have to check with him first, but I think he'll be cool." Joey speaks back into the apartment.
Joey walks into the hallway and closes the door behind him.
The scene changes to an exterior view of a glass building where people are visible exercising on treadmills. The setting then changes to an indoor hallway as Chandler and Ross walk side-by-side.
"Hey, now, remember what we talked about. You got to be strong." Ross stops Chandler, holding Chandler's arm.
"Yes, yes." Chandler replies, nodding.
"One more time." Ross speaks, raising his index finger.
"Hey, don't you want a washboard stomach and rock hard pecs?" Ross asks, pointing at Chandler.
"No, I want a flabby gut and saggy man breasts." Chandler replies, his volume rising slightly on the final phrase.
Ross nods, smiling.
"Good. That's good. Okay." Ross speaks, patting Chandler's arm.
Chandler and Ross turn and walk down the hallway toward a reception desk.
A male Gym Employee sits behind the circular desk. Chandler approaches him while Ross stands a few steps behind.
"I want to quit the gym." Chandler speaks to the employee.
The Gym Employee looks up.
"You want to quit?" The employee asks.
Chandler turns to look at Ross. Ross nods and gestures slightly toward the desk. Chandler turns back.
"I want to quit the gym." Chandler repeats.
The employee leans forward, resting his hands on the desk.
"You do realize you won't have access to our new full service Swedish spa?" The employee asks, spreading his hands.
Chandler's mouth falls open slightly. Behind him, Ross shakes his head and snaps his fingers, pointing at Chandler. Chandler blinks and shakes his head.
"I want to quit the gym." Chandler speaks, louder than his previous line.
The employee sits back.
"Okay, Dave in the membership office handles quitters." The employee points to his left.
Chandler nods and turns away from the desk. Ross begins to follow him.
"Excuse me. Are you a member?" The employee asks, looking at Ross.
Ross stops and points to himself.
"Me?" Ross asks.
The employee nods.
"No." Ross answers.
"Sorry. Members only." The employee speaks, gesturing toward the exit.
Ross looks at Chandler, then shrugs. Chandler stares back at him.
"I want to quit the gym." Chandler tells Ross, leaning forward.
"Okay, man." Ross grabs Chandler's shoulders.
"Be strong." Ross speaks, shaking Chandler slightly.
Chandler walks away, raising a fist. Ross turns back to face the reception desk.
"MMM." Ross hums lightly, standing near the desk.
The employee looks up at Ross.
"So are you a member of any gym?" The employee asks.
"No, and I'm not going to be. So you can save your little speech." Ross speaks, holding his hand up.
"Okay, no problem." The employee smiles slightly, holding up two fingers.
"Could you come here for a second?" The employee says, looking past Ross.
A blonde woman wearing a high-cut grey leotard walks into view and stops beside Ross.
"Hi, I'm Maria." The woman smiles at Ross, placing her hands on her hips.
Ross stares at Maria, his mouth slightly open, and smiles.

The scene changes to a street view showing an awning that reads "HEALING HANDS INC. Licensed Massage Therapists". The setting then changes to a dimly lit massage room, where Phoebe rubs the back of a man lying face-down on a massage table.
A view through the table's face cradle shows Phoebe's bare feet with red nail polish shifting on the carpet.
The view changes to show the man resting his head in the face cradle, his eyes open.
"Wow, you have really pretty feet." The man speaks.
The view returns to Phoebe standing beside the table, massaging his back.
"These old things?" Phoebe asks, smiling.
The man turns his head slightly on the cradle.
"Would you mind spending some time on my sciatic area? It's been killing me today." The man asks, his eyes closed and his face tightening.
Phoebe stops massaging and looks down at him.
"By sciatic, you mean the..." Phoebe speaks, pointing down toward his lower back.
"towel covered portion yeah," The man answers.
Phoebe nods.
"sure. Yeah, no, I can do that. Yeah, because usually the muscles in the sciatic area can get real nice and" Phoebe speaks, clapping her hands lightly.
Phoebe pulls the edge of the pink towel upward, exposing his lower back, and smiles.
"tight." Phoebe finishes her sentence, looking down.
Phoebe smiles widely, looking at his back, then claps her hands together and looks up.
"Yeah. So tell me, Rick, how'd you injure the area?" Phoebe asks, gesturing briefly.
"16 hours sit in for Greenpeace." Rick answers.
Phoebe's eyes widen and she opens her mouth in a smile. She pulls the towel slightly higher and continues to look down at him.
In a massage room, a man lies face down on a table with a pink towel covering his lower half. Phoebe stands beside him and lowers her head toward his back.
The man suddenly twitches, lifting his upper body to look back at Phoebe over his shoulder.
The man asks, "Did you just bite me?" Phoebe stands upright, pulling her hands back, and answers, "No."
Phoebe stands still. The man lowers his upper body back onto the table. The setting changes to an exterior view of a city street lined with brick buildings, where a yellow taxi drives past.
In an apartment hallway, Joey walks up to a brown door labeled "MAINTENANCE SUPERVISOR" and knocks. Mr. Treeger opens the door, wearing dark blue coveralls.

Mr. Treeger asks, "What?" Joey, raising both hands with open palms, says, "Please don't kick Monica and Rachel out. This wasn't their fault. It was mine." He places his hands on his chest. Mr. Treeger asks, "You want me to kick you guys out instead?"
Joey lowers his arms and looks slightly downward.
Joey raises his hands, palms up, and says, "No, you can't do that. Where would the chick and the duck live?"
Mr. Treeger stares at Joey with slightly furrowed eyebrows.
Mr. Treeger asks, "You have pets?"
Joey shakes his head, waving his hands, and says, "No,"
Joey continues to shake his head and waves his hands, glancing down before looking back at Mr. Treeger.
Joey says, "those are nicknames." while gesturing with one hand.
Pointing to his chest, Joey says, "Yeah. I'm the chick," then points his thumb over his shoulder, "and Chandler's the duck."
Mr. Treeger looks at Joey with a steady gaze.
Mr. Treeger says, "I would have thought it was the other way around."
Joey shrugs slightly, lowering his gaze. Mr. Treeger begins to close the door, but Joey steps forward and pushes it back open.
Joey says, "Come on, man."
Spreading his arms outward, Joey says, "Just let the girls stay."
Joey clasps his hands together and says, "I'll do whatever you want."
Mr. Treeger stops moving the door and looks at Joey.
Mr. Treeger asks, "Really?"
Mr. Treeger asks, "You do?" Joey nods quickly and claps his hands, saying, "Yeah, yeah, absolutely."
Mr. Treeger looks at Joey with a neutral expression.
Mr. Treeger starts, "I got something you can-" Joey interrupts, leaning forward slightly, "what? What is it?"
Mr. Treeger rubs his fingers together, looking down briefly, then back up at Joey.
Mr. Treeger asks, "Can you be my dancing partner?"
Joey stares at Mr. Treeger with his mouth slightly open, then slowly raises his right index finger.
Holding both hands up with palms facing outward, Joey asks, "That's not prison lingo, is it?"
Mr. Treeger looks at Joey. The setting changes to a brightly lit office, where Chandler sits in a chair across from a desk.
Looking off-screen to his right, Chandler says, "I want to quit the gym."
A gym employee in black athletic wear sits in a swivel chair behind the desk, facing Chandler.
Gesturing with his right hand, the employee asks, "Now, can you honestly tell me that you're 100% satisfied with your body?"
Chandler points his right index finger forward and says, "Yes, most of the time."
Chandler shrugs and adds, "I mean, sure, I have my bad days."
Chandler smiles briefly and gestures near his face, saying, "But then I remember what a cute smile I have."
The gym employee raises a finger and says, "Look, we were voted best equipped gym in New York two years running. Do"
The employee continues, "you really want to give that up?" Chandler quickly leans forward and answers, "Yes."
Chandler says, "I hate it here."
Chandler clenches his fists slightly. The employee looks at him.
Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "Everything that you have in here is very heavy."
A telephone rings. The gym employee closes his eyes briefly, presses his lips together, and reaches for the phone on his desk.
The employee lifts the receiver to his ear and says, "Yeah."
The employee listens to the phone, his gaze shifting to the side.
Looking toward the office door, the employee says, "Real Millie."
Chandler leans forward, resting his chin on his hand, looking at the employee.
Hanging up the phone, the gym employee says, "Okay."
The employee turns his chair back toward Chandler, resting his forearms on the desk.
The employee asks, "You don't want to make your friend work out alone, do you?"
Chandler frowns and asks, "What friend?" The employee points his thumb over his shoulder and replies, "Your friend Ross just joined."
The employee stands up, turning toward the window behind him, and pulls the horizontal blinds open.
Through the window, Ross is visible sitting on a stationary bike in the gym. A woman in a black swimsuit stands next to him, resting her hands on her hips.
Ross smiles widely and talks to the woman, wiping his forehead with a white towel. Chandler remains seated, staring out the window with his mouth slightly open.
